page: Amazon Web Services i3.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance designed for high I/O workloads. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor featuring 32 virtual CPUs and 16 physical cores running at 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing the Xen hypervisor. This instance includes 244.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.62 GB per core, and features a 45 MB L3 cache.

A key hardware characteristic of the i3.8xlarge is its bundled local storage, consisting of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. This massive local storage is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The instance does not include any GPU hardware.

In terms of performance, the server exhibits distinct tradeoffs. Single-core processing is a weak point, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core benchmarks placing it in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show weak to poor performance. However, multi-core performance is more robust, scoring in the middle 50% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark multi-core tests. Memory read bandwidth is average, though large-block memory write bandwidth is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3.8xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a viable option for workloads that require high-speed local data access without the premium of external storage attachments. However, the older Intel Xeon architecture presents a tradeoff in single-threaded efficiency compared to newer generations.

This hardware profile makes the i3.8xlarge well-suited for storage-intensive and database workloads, such as NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and data warehousing, where high local NVMe capacity and average multi-core processing are required.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.8xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Xen hypervisor, it includes 244.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core CPU benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks perform poorly, landing in the bottom 10% to 25%, its multi-core capabilities and memory read bandwidth consistently rank in the average tier. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy applications by bundling massive local NVMe capacity directly with the instance. The i3.8xlarge is highly suited for database operations, NoSQL systems, and high-throughput local storage workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.8xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 244.0 GB of RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and storage workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized instance bundling massive local NVMe SSD capacity with balanced multi-core performance for data-intensive database workloads.

7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled
244.0 GB of system memory with 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth

Suited for NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and storage-heavy workloads
